skill.name: ai-engineer
skill.description: Build production-ready LLM applications, RAG systems, AI agents, vector search, multimodal workflows, and enterprise AI integrations.
skill.summary: Design and implement reliable LLM applications, RAG systems, and AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and jailbreak-keyword patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are defensive or descriptive Markdown references, not executable code, command usage, network access, or prompt injection. No confirmed malicious behavior or hidden semantic risk was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are natural-language references to AI architecture, system design, and production practices. They do not mention or implement weak cryptographic algorithms and contain no executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown instruction document with no crypto implementation or configuration. The flagged terms appear in benign phrases such as architecture and design guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss protecting sensitive data, BM25 hybrid search, and an example RAG request. They do not instruct collection of host details, credentials, network inventory, or environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is AI safety and retrieval-system design. I found no commands, scripts, or data exfiltration paths connected to reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Known Jailbreak Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged term appears in a defensive safety capability for jailbreak detection, content filtering, and bias mitigation. It does not ask the model to bypass instructions or skip security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is defensive prompt safety guidance. No override language, fake system message, or instruction to ignore prior instructions was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable LLM systems that work beyond prototypes. This skill guides architecture, RAG design, agents, safety controls, monitoring, and production rollout.
actual_capabilities: Designs LLM application architectures with model routing, caching, and fallback strategies.
Plans advanced RAG systems with chunking, hybrid search, reranking, and evaluation.
Guides AI agent workflows using tools, memory, orchestration, and monitoring.
Recommends safety controls for prompt injection, PII, content moderation, and governance.
Defines production rollout plans with testing, observability, cost controls, and latency targets.
Supports multimodal AI patterns for document, image, audio, and video workflows.
limitations: Requires access to project requirements, data sources, and deployment constraints.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or security review for regulated deployments.
Cannot verify vendor pricing, model availability, or API changes without current references.
May need additional domain experts for specialized data or safety policies.

use_cases.title: Plan an Enterprise RAG System
use_cases.description: Create a retrieval architecture with indexing, hybrid search, reranking, evaluation, and governance for internal knowledge.

use_cases.title: Build an AI Product Feature
use_cases.description: Design a chatbot, summarizer, copilot, or workflow assistant with clear reliability and cost requirements.

use_cases.title: Harden an Existing LLM App
use_cases.description: Add monitoring, safety checks, prompt injection defenses, fallback behavior, and staged rollout plans.

prompt_templates.title: Define the AI Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define an LLM feature for my product. Ask for goals, users, data sources, constraints, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design a RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production RAG pipeline for these documents and users. Include ingestion, chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, evaluation, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Architect an AI Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AI agent architecture for this workflow. Include tools, memory, guardrails, escalation paths, observability, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this LLM application design for production readiness. Focus on safety, latency, cost, evaluation, incident response, and rollout risk.

output_examples.input: Design a support chatbot for internal policy documents.
output: A RAG architecture with document ingestion, metadata filters, hybrid retrieval, reranking, and answer citation requirements.
A safety plan covering prompt injection checks, PII handling, moderation, and human escalation.
An evaluation plan with groundedness, retrieval recall, latency, and unresolved-ticket metrics.

output_examples.input: Improve cost and latency for our LLM workflow.
output: A routing strategy that uses smaller models for simple tasks and larger models for complex cases.
Caching recommendations for embeddings, repeated prompts, and stable responses.
Monitoring guidance for token usage, timeout rates, quality drift, and fallback frequency.

output_examples.input: Plan a multimodal document analysis system.
output: A pipeline for OCR, layout extraction, table handling, image understanding, and text normalization.
A retrieval design that preserves page, section, and source references.
A testing approach with representative documents, edge cases, and manual review checkpoints.

best_practices: Start with explicit success metrics for quality, latency, cost, and safety.
Add evaluation datasets before broad rollout so regressions are visible.
Keep prompts, models, retrieval settings, and safety policies versioned.
anti_patterns: Do not send sensitive data to external models without approval and controls.
Do not rely on one prompt as the only safety boundary.
Do not ship agent tools without permissions, audit logs, and failure handling.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build LLM applications, RAG systems, AI agents, vector search workflows, and multimodal AI features.

faq.question: Can it choose the best model for my app?
faq.answer: It can recommend model selection criteria based on quality, latency, cost, privacy, tool use, and deployment needs.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: It can guide production implementation patterns, testing, monitoring, error handling, and rollout planning.

faq.question: Is it useful for safety reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers prompt injection defenses, PII handling, content filtering, governance, and adversarial testing.

faq.question: Does it require a specific AI provider?
faq.answer: No. It references multiple model providers and local deployment options for different requirements.

faq.question: When should I not use it?
faq.answer: Do not use it for pure data science, unrelated UI work, or projects without AI data and deployment context.
